ticket_title: Positive, negative and screenshot proof for Signal, Analyst Workbench and Internal Draft Governance

scope.validated_contract: Signal, Analyst Workbench and Internal Draft Governance process-first implementation slice

dependencies_satisfied_by_commits.summary: Domain gap report present and re-verified
Machine-readable analyst draft implementation contract created
S033 analyst signal area entry contract and route proof added
P0 process coverage matrix statuses updated without completion overclaim

positive_path_proof.evidence: Signal intake creates scoped internal queue state and audit without client-visible advice.
Analyst workbench triage advances internal lifecycle state with audit and no client visibility.
Action items are created from signal/workbench state without releasing advice.
Evidence gaps route to request or blocked state and do not become client-visible sufficiency claims.
Internal draft governance uses first-class Prisma models and forbids legacy Recommendation-field fallback.

negative_path_proof.evidence: Invalid source, confidence, severity or scope is denied before workflow state is created.
Signal intake and triage remain internal until approval and release gates.
Internal draft payload fields are excluded from client-safe and export-safe claims.
S034 exposes concise internal proof without client-safe, export-ready, advisor-approved or compliance-release overclaim.
S035 requires trigger audit proof while keeping audit persistence and draft payload internal-only.

screenshot_proof.assertion: Area entry fits without page scroll and exposes a single product-native workbench entry.
Review queue/detail step surface is above the fold with no page scroll, no oversized summary banner and no internal proof strip.
Trigger review keeps selected object, decision context, blocker and next action visible without page scroll.

recorded_orchestration_retries.resolution: Both commands were rerun sequentially and passed.

mission_card.mission: Prove EPIC-09 as a bounded process-first Signal, Analyst Workbench and Internal Draft Governance implementation slice without claiming full domain closure.
success_criteria: Negative no-overclaim and client-safe boundaries pass
S033-S035 screenshots show compact operational surfaces
Coverage matrix reflects proof while blocking unsafe completion claims

facts: Implementation subtasks A-C were already committed and re-verified.
Implementation subtask D updated the P0 matrix in the current slice.
No EPIC-09 process step is marked implemented; all 60 AREA-04 steps are partial.

contradictions_resolved.contradiction: The UI must become compact without hiding proof and blockers.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Use concise step/audit surfaces and test DOM proof boundaries.

contradictions_resolved.contradiction: The domain slice needs acceptance but full safety closure is not complete.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Accept the bounded implementation slice while the matrix blocks domain closure.

contradictions_resolved.contradiction: Internal AI draft state must be useful to analysts but invisible to clients.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: First-class internal draft spine plus payload visibility contract and negative UI tests.

killed_branches.option: Mark DOMAIN-D/E implemented because route/UI proof exists.
killed_branches.reason: Rejected as overclaim; backend lifecycle and audit failure proof remain incomplete.

killed_branches.option: Leave the 30 specified-only EPIC-09 steps untouched.
killed_branches.reason: Rejected as stale; contract, route and negative proof now create honest partial coverage.

killed_branches.option: Add new routes or schemas for QA closure.
killed_branches.reason: Rejected under True-UX route/scope discipline; existing S033-S035 surfaces are sufficient for this slice.

ethics_and_fairness.exit_or_stop_condition: Any target QA failure would block EPIC-09 acceptance.

learning_log: AREA-04 can now be treated as a coherent process-first slice, but not a closed domain.
The coverage matrix is the right place to keep bold cleanup honest: remove stale specified-only status where proof exists, but keep implementation closure blocked.
Playwright acceptance should run these QA axes sequentially because parallel runs collide on the Next dev server.

final_recommendation.rationale: EPIC-09 target behavior, negative safety and viewport proof passed, while the matrix prevents unsafe full-domain completion claims.
final_recommendation.next_ticket: EPIC-17-QA-01 or the next ordered masterplan epic after EPIC-09
